evals.prompt: I've been setting up this IoT project with BMAD and all the planning docs are done. Sprint status file exists with 3 epics, first one is in-progress with story 1.2 ready-for-dev. Go ahead and auto implement the rest.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator reads sprint-status.yaml, detects Epic 1 in-progress, skips story 1.1 (done), starts story 1.2 at Step 3 (Develop), creates team, spawns story-developer sub-agent.

assertions.name: reads_sprint_status_first
assertions.description: Orchestrator reads sprint-status.yaml before doing anything else

assertions.name: skips_done_stories
assertions.description: Skips story 1.1 which is already done

assertions.name: resumes_at_correct_step
assertions.description: Story 1.2 with ready-for-dev status starts at Step 3 (Develop), not Step 1

assertions.name: creates_team
assertions.description: Calls TeamCreate before spawning sub-agents

assertions.name: spawns_team_subagent
assertions.description: Uses Agent tool with team_name parameter, not a disposable sub-agent

evals.prompt: Start the automatic implementation phase. Note: there is no sprint-status.yaml file yet, I only have the planning docs ready.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator checks for sprint-status.yaml, finds it missing, invokes bmad-help skill to suggest next actions, reports to user and stops without entering main loop.

assertions.name: checks_file_existence
assertions.description: Checks if sprint-status.yaml exists before proceeding

assertions.name: calls_bmad_help
assertions.description: Invokes bmad-help skill when sprint-status.yaml is missing

assertions.name: does_not_enter_main_loop
assertions.description: Does NOT create a team or spawn sub-agents

evals.prompt: What's the status of the implementation? How many stories are done?
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator reads sprint-status.yaml, summarizes progress (epics done/in-progress, stories done/remaining), reports any blockers, and does NOT continue the main loop.

assertions.name: reads_status_file
assertions.description: Reads sprint-status.yaml for current state

assertions.name: reports_summary
assertions.description: Provides a summary of epic and story progress

assertions.name: does_not_continue_loop
assertions.description: Does NOT enter the main loop or spawn any sub-agents

evals.prompt: auto implement - all epics and stories in the sprint-status.yaml are marked as done already
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator reads sprint-status.yaml, detects all work is complete, invokes bmad-help for next action suggestions, reports completion and stops.

assertions.name: detects_all_done
assertions.description: Correctly identifies that all epics and stories are done

assertions.name: calls_bmad_help_on_completion
assertions.description: Invokes bmad-help skill to suggest next actions

assertions.name: does_not_enter_main_loop
assertions.description: Does NOT create a team or start working on stories

evals.prompt: begin phase 4 implementation. The sub-agent developing story 2.1 reported that it needs me to manually create an AWS S3 bucket and configure IAM permissions before it can proceed.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator instructs the sub-agent to investigate automation first (AWS CLI, CloudFormation, Terraform, CDK). Only halts for user if automation is truly impossible.

assertions.name: investigates_automation
assertions.description: Sub-agent or orchestrator investigates if the manual task can be automated (AWS CLI, IaC tools)

assertions.name: does_not_immediately_halt
assertions.description: Does NOT immediately halt and ask the user - tries automation first

evals.prompt: I have a tech spec for fixing the login validation bug. The spec is at _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/tech-spec-fix-login-validation.md. Go ahead and implement it.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator detects Quick Flow (user provided tech-spec path), reads the spec file, creates team, spawns quick-developer sub-agent with bmad-quick-dev skill pointing to the spec file. Does NOT look for sprint-status.yaml or epics.

assertions.name: detects_quick_flow
assertions.description: Routes to Quick Flow instead of Phase 4 when user provides a tech-spec

assertions.name: reads_tech_spec
assertions.description: Reads the provided tech-spec file before starting implementation

assertions.name: skips_phase4_startup
assertions.description: Does NOT check for sprint-status.yaml or epics.md

assertions.name: spawns_quick_developer
assertions.description: Spawns a sub-agent that invokes bmad-quick-dev with the spec path

assertions.name: creates_team
assertions.description: Creates bmad-auto team before spawning sub-agents

evals.prompt: I need to refactor the UserService to use the repository pattern instead of direct database calls. It's a focused change across 3 files in src/users/. Can you spec it out and implement it?
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator detects Quick Flow (small, well-understood change without sprint-status.yaml), starts at Quick Flow Step 1 (Create Tech-Spec) by spawning quick-spec-creator sub-agent with bmad-quick-spec. After spec approval, proceeds to Step 2 (Implement).

assertions.name: detects_quick_flow_from_description
assertions.description: Routes to Quick Flow based on the description of a small, focused change

assertions.name: starts_with_spec_creation
assertions.description: Starts at Quick Flow Step 1 (Create Tech-Spec) since no spec exists

assertions.name: spawns_spec_creator
assertions.description: Spawns quick-spec-creator sub-agent using bmad-quick-spec skill

assertions.name: includes_user_request_in_prompt
assertions.description: Includes the user's change description in the sub-agent prompt

evals.prompt: quick dev — there's already a tech-spec somewhere for the auth token refresh fix, I created it last session. just find it and implement it.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator detects Quick Flow (user said 'quick dev'), searches _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/ for matching tech-spec-*.md files. If found, proceeds to Quick Flow Step 2 (Implement). If not found, asks user for the file path.

assertions.name: detects_quick_flow_from_keyword
assertions.description: Routes to Quick Flow based on 'quick dev' keyword

assertions.name: searches_for_existing_spec
assertions.description: Searches _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/ for matching tech-spec files

assertions.name: does_not_create_new_spec
assertions.description: Does NOT start spec creation — tries to find existing spec first

evals.prompt:
I want to add rate limiting to the /api/users endpoint. It should use a sliding window algorithm with Redis. Here's my tech spec:

## Problem
No rate limiting on user API endpoints.

## Solution
Add sliding window rate limiter using ioredis.

## Tasks
1. Install ioredis dependency
2. Create RateLimiterService in src/common/rate-limiter.service.ts
3. Create RateLimiterGuard in src/common/rate-limiter.guard.ts
4. Apply guard to UsersController
5. Add unit tests for RateLimiterService

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a client sends 100 requests within 60 seconds, when the 101st request arrives, then return 429 Too Many Requests
- Given the sliding window expires, when a new request arrives, then allow it through
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator detects Quick Flow (user provided inline tech-spec), saves the spec as tech-spec-rate-limiting.md in _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/, then proceeds to Quick Flow Step 2 (Implement).

assertions.name: detects_inline_spec
assertions.description: Recognizes the inline content as a tech-spec and routes to Quick Flow

assertions.name: saves_spec_to_file
assertions.description: Saves the inline spec as a tech-spec-*.md file in the correct directory

assertions.name: skips_spec_creation_step
assertions.description: Proceeds directly to Quick Flow Step 2, not Step 1

assertions.name: spawns_quick_developer
assertions.description: Spawns quick-developer sub-agent to implement the saved spec

evals.prompt: The quick-developer sub-agent reported that the rate limiting change is actually much bigger than expected — it needs changes to the API gateway, a new shared Redis cluster config, and updates to 12 microservices. It's recommending we do proper architecture planning first.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator detects scope escalation, reports to user with options: light escalation (more detailed spec) or heavy escalation (switch to full BMAD Phases 1-4). Does NOT force either path — waits for user decision. Emphasizes that existing work carries forward.

assertions.name: detects_scope_escalation
assertions.description: Recognizes the sub-agent's report as a scope escalation signal

assertions.name: offers_escalation_options
assertions.description: Presents both light and heavy escalation options to the user

assertions.name: preserves_existing_work
assertions.description: Communicates that the tech-spec carries forward — no work is lost

assertions.name: waits_for_user_decision
assertions.description: Does NOT automatically switch flows — waits for user to choose
